title: Proteomic Protocol for Tibetan Hulless Barley Under Osmotic Stress
author: Yulin Wang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbusinwe

steps.title: Weigh 1-g subsample and homogenized by...
steps.instruction: Weigh 1-g subsample and homogenized by grinding in liquid nitrogen, moved the powdered samples to 50 cm3 tubes;

steps.title: Add 25 cm3 precooled acetone (-20...
steps.instruction: Add 25 cm3 precooled acetone (-20 °C) containing 10% (v/v) trichloroacetic acid (TCA) and 10 mM dithiothreitol (DTT); 25 mL, precooled acetone (-20 °C)

steps.title: Mix for 2 min, the homogenate...
steps.instruction: Mix for 2 min, the homogenate was precipitated overnight at -20 °C and then centrifuged (20,000×g, 4 °C) for 30 min; Overnight, homogenate at -20 °C 20000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 20 cm3...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 20 cm3 chilled acetone (-20 °C) and left at -20 °C for 30 min followed by centrifugation (20,000×g, 4 °C) for 30 min, repeat this step for twice; 00:30:00 20000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes 20000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and add lysis...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and add lysis buffer (4% SDS, 100 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM DTT, pH 8.0) in the precipitate;

steps.title: Sonicate for 5 min at 60...
steps.instruction: Sonicate for 5 min at 60 W (5 s sonication followed by 10 s break); 00:05:00, sonicate at 60 W

steps.title: Centrifuge for 30 min (20,000×g, 20...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 30 min (20,000×g, 20 °C) and collect the supernatant; 20000 x g, 20 °C, 30 minutes

steps.title: Estimate the protein concentration using a...
steps.instruction: Estimate the protein concentration using a bicinchoninic acid (BCA) protein assay kit (Beyotime Institute of Biotechnology, China)

steps.title: Mix up to protein extract with...
steps.instruction: Mix up to protein extract with 200 mm3 UA buffer (8 M urea, 150 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0) and centrifuged at 14,000 g at 20°C for 40 min. 200 µL, UA buffer (8 M urea, 150 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0) 14000 x g, 20 °C, 40 minutes

steps.title: Add 200 mm3 of UA to...
steps.instruction: Add 200 mm3 of UA to the filter unit and centrifuge at 14,000 x g for 40 min. Discard the flow-through form the collection tube. Repeat this step twice. 200 µL, UA 14000 x g, °C, 40 minutes

steps.title: Add 100 mm3 IAM solution (10...
steps.instruction: Add 100 mm3 IAM solution (10 mM IAM in UA buffer) and incubate for 30 min. 100 µL, IAM solution (10 mM IAM in UA buffer) 00:30:00, incubate

steps.title: Centrifuge the filter units at 14,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the filter units at 14,000 x g for 30 min. 14000 x g, °C, 30 minutes

steps.title: Add 100 mm3 of UA to...
steps.instruction: Add 100 mm3 of UA to the filter unit and centrifuge at 14,000 x g for 40 min. Repeat this step twice. 100 µL, UA 14000 x g, °C, 40 minutes 14000 x g, °C, 40 minutes

steps.title: Add 100 mm3 of ABC (0
steps.instruction: Add 100 mm3 of ABC (0.05 M NH4HCO3 in water) and centrifuged at 14,000 g. Repeat this step twice. 100 µL, ABC (0.05 M NH4HCO3 in water) 14000 x g, °C, 40 minutes 14000 x g, °C, 40 minutes

steps.title: Add 40 mm3 ABC with trypsin...
steps.instruction: Add 40 mm3 ABC with trypsin (Promega, USA) and incubated for 18 h at 37°C; 40 µL, ABC with trypsin (Promega, USA) 18:00:00, incubate at 37°C

steps.title: Transfer the filter units to new collection tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer the filter units to new collection tubes.

steps.title: Separate peptides using an LC-20AB HPLC...
steps.instruction: Separate peptides using an LC-20AB HPLC system (Shimadzu, Kyoto, Japan) with a high-pH reversed-phase (high-pH RP) column (Phenomenon, Torrance, CA). Peptides were eluted at a flow rate of 0.8 cm3/min. Buffer A consisted of 10 mM ammonium acetate (pH 10.0), and buffer B consisted of 10 mM ammonium acetate and 90% v/v acetonitrile (pH 10.0). The following gradient was applied to perform separation: 100% buffer A for 40 min, 0-5% buffer B for 3 min, 5-35% buffer B for 30 min, 35-70% buffer B for 10 min, 70-75% buffer B for 10 min, 75-100% buffer B for 7 min, 100% buffer B for 15 min and, finally, 100% buffer A for 15 min. The elution process was monitored by measuring absorbance at 214 nm, and fractions were collected every 75 s. Finally, collected fractions (approximately 40) were combined into 12 pools. Each fraction was concentrated via vacuum centrifugation and reconstituted in 40 mm3 of 0.1% v/v formic acid.

steps.title: Centrifuge the filter units at 14,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the filter units at 14,000 x g for 10 min and collect the filtration. 14000 x g, °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Peptides were separated with a Dionex...
steps.instruction: Peptides were separated with a Dionex UltiMate 3000 RSLCnano system with an Acclaim PepMap C18 (3 μm, 100 Å, 75 μm x 50 cm) and emitted into a Thermo Q-Exactive HF tandem mass spectrometer. Solvent A was 0.1% formic acid in water, while solvent B was 0.1% formic acid in 98% acetonitrile. For each injection, 3 mm3 (approximately 3 μg) was loaded and eluted using a 90-minute gradient from 5 to 35% B followed by a 40 min washing gradient. Data were acquired using either data-dependent acquisition (DDA) or data-independent acquisition (DIA).
